title: Aureline telemetry, diagnostics, and support-export schema registry entry
description: Boundary schema for one row in the telemetry, crash, diagnostics, support-bundle, metering/usage-export, offboarding-packet, and CLI/headless schema registry seeded in artifacts/governance/consent_ledger_seed.yaml and described in docs/governance/telemetry_and_support_schema_registry.md. Each row names one governed payload family so telemetry, crash, diagnostic, support-export, metering, offboarding, and CLI/headless schemas stay distinguishable even when they share transport, redaction, or export plumbing. A row carries owner, schema version, consent class, endpoint class, retention note, per-build-flavor default posture, compatibility horizon, downgrade rules, the default UX-telemetry exclusions the family inherits, explicit sibling-family linkage, and the local-only-lane posture reviewers need to spot coercive or ambiguous defaults. Adding a new family_class, consent_class, endpoint_class, build_flavor, build_flavor_posture, downgrade_action, telemetry_exclusion_class, or local_only_lane_posture value is additive-minor and requires a schema_registry_schema_version bump; repurposing an existing value is breaking and requires a new decision row.

schema_registry_schema_version.description: Integer schema version for this registry-entry vocabulary. Current value is 1. Bumped only on breaking payload changes; additive-optional fields do not bump this version.

reviewable_sentence.description: Short human-readable sentence intended for reviewer surfaces, docs, manifests, or audit rows.

path_ref.description: Repo-relative path or document reference. Consumers treat it as opaque text.

stable_dot_id.description: Dot-separated stable identifier; used for the row id and sibling-link references.

owner_ref.description: Stable lower_snake identifier for the owning team, role, or governance body (for example telemetry_governance, support_export, boundary_governance, finance_controls).

record_class_id_token.description: Record-class id from artifacts/governance/record_class_registry.yaml that this payload family maps to.

family_class.description: Top-level family this registry row governs. Registry consumers key off family_class to separate analytics, support manifests, crash/diagnostic payloads, metering/usage exports, offboarding packets, and CLI/headless schemas even when plumbing is shared.

consent_class.description: How consent is established for this payload family. The class names who decides and under what posture; it does not name individual settings keys or UX copy.

endpoint_class.description: Where the payload may travel by default. Parallel to record-class scope_posture but specific to the transport or export surface that carries the schema.

build_flavor.description: Build flavor token, aligned with schemas/governance/evidence_packet_header channel_context and schemas/support/support_bundle install_channel_class.

build_flavor_posture.description: Default posture the build flavor ships with. admin_policy_gated names a flavor where the managed admin decides posture but the client does not default to a specific enable/disable state; admin_forced_enabled and admin_forced_disabled name a flavor where the managed admin has already forced one side. Reviewers check whether a flavor's posture would be coercive or ambiguous on a local-only lane and must land a justification when it is.

min_readable_version.description: Oldest schema_version readers must still parse into the current shape.

min_writable_version.description: Oldest schema_version writers may still emit when a consumer requests compatibility output.

downgrade_action.description: Typed action a reader or writer must take when it encounters a schema_version outside the supported horizon.

telemetry_exclusion_class.description: Classes of raw or sensitive payload that UX telemetry, crash metadata, usage exports, and CLI/headless captures never include by default.

exclusion_override_policy.description: How an exclusion may be overridden, if at all. Anything other than never_permitted must cite a separately-reviewed approval.

permitted_by_separate_review_refs.description: Separately-reviewed schemas, ADRs, or waivers that explicitly permit the exclusion class. Required when override_policy names a reviewed opt-in or opt-out.

sibling_linkage_class.description: How this family relates to another family when they must reference each other or must be kept from embedding one another.

canonical_family_class.description: The family this row is canonical for. A support-bundle manifest row, for example, remains separate even when it shares transport with analytics or offboarding schemas.

must_not_conflate_with_family_classes.description: Families whose plumbing may be shared but whose policy or manifest shape must not be collapsed into this row.

local_only_lane_posture.description: Posture reviewers audit to spot payload families whose defaults would be coercive or ambiguous on a local-only lane. Anything other than local_only_safe_default must carry a justification note.

schema_version_uri.description: Stable $id or version URI the schema document publishes. Consumers cite this verbatim.

record_class_id_refs.description: Record-class ids from artifacts/governance/record_class_registry.yaml that govern the retention, export, delete, and offboarding posture this payload family inherits.

redaction_artifact_refs.description: Canonical redaction artifacts the family inherits.

retention_artifact_refs.description: Canonical retention, delete-honesty, or hold artifacts the family inherits.

source_docs.description: Repo docs a reviewer should consult when changing the row.

related_schema_refs.description: Optional sibling boundary schemas cited by the family.

entry_id.description: Stable dot-separated id for this registry entry. Mint a new id rather than reusing an old one for a different family.

title.description: Short human-readable title for the entry.

description.description: One-paragraph statement of what the family is, what it does not cover, and how it is distinguished from siblings it shares plumbing with.

retention_note.description: Short reviewable sentence stating how long the payload lives, what closes the window, and which record-class retention posture it inherits.

build_flavor_default_posture.description: Per-build-flavor default posture. Any flavor the product is shipped under must appear here so the reviewer can spot posture drift across channels.

downgrade_rules.description: Ordered downgrade rules readers and writers apply when they encounter a schema_version outside the active horizon.

default_ux_payload_exclusions.description: Default exclusion posture for raw code, prompts, repo names, file paths, clipboard contents, full search queries, voice audio, and similar sensitive payloads. Every exclusion carries an override_policy that names how it may be widened if at all.

sibling_family_links.description: Explicit linkage rows when this family must reference a sibling family or must be kept from embedding one. Empty when the family stands alone.

local_only_lane_note.description: Justification that makes the local-only-lane posture reviewable. Anything other than local_only_safe_default must explain why the posture is honest rather than coercive or ambiguous.

notes.description: Optional additional note for future consumers.
